title: Vue.js Event Handling Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of v-on (@ shorthand), method handlers, and event modifiers.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the prefix/shorthand for listening to events in Vue.js?
Which syntax in Vue templates would you use for a button click event?
What is the purpose of event modifiers like .stop and .prevent in Vue?
options: To perform common DOM operations (like stopPropagation and preventDefault) within the template.
To only stop the app from running.
To add CSS styles to the element.
They are just for performance only.

questions.question: Which modifier would you use to ensure an event handler is only triggered once?
Can you pass multiple arguments to an event handler method in Vue (e.g. @click='warn(message, $event)')?
options: Yes, by passing them as a list.
No, only one argument is allowed.
Only in older versions of Vue.
